feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Process Integration
feed.subtitle: SAP Process Integration Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: ChainVerifier: EC signed SHA256withRSA server certificate server certificate not capable for..
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I am facing the following issue with establishing a connection using a RestReceiver. Unfortunately, there is an issue with the certificate. The following error is appearing:

ChainVerifier: EC signed SHA256withRSA server certificate server certificate not capable for ECDHE_ECDSA key exchange algorithm!

ChainVerifier: EC signed SHA256withRSA server certificate server certificate not capable for ECDHE_ECDSA key exchange algorithm!

There is an SAP note regarding this matter. It explains:

"Contact the administrators of the target server to create a certificate signed with the ECDSA encryption algorithm." 

3057117 - Peer-Zertifikat von ChainVerifier abgelehnt - EC-signiertes SHA256withRSA-Serverzertifikat Serverzertifikat nicht für ECDHE_ECDSA-Schlüsselaustauschalgorithmus geeignet! - SAP for Me

However, are there any other solutions available?

On the internet, it is mentioned that it is indeed possible to use a SHA256withRSA signature with the ECDHE_ECDSA key exchange algorithm.

Do you have any insights?
I would greatly appreciate your input.

Best Regards,
Mohammad

entry.title: Cancel job in SAP PO 7.5 is not considering 4 year old messages
entry.content:
Hi Friends, 

Good day.

Our PIPO system version is 7.5.

I try to schedule a cancel job to cancel 4-year-old messages. (There are huge messages are in holding state in 2020). When I scheduled this job, I entered Older Than (Hours) parameter values as 35040. (365 * 4 * 24). However, the job is not considering 4-year-old messages it seems. After successful job run, the job log says, "Number of "EOIO" messages to be cancelled: 0 (in 0 sequences). 2020 holding messages are not cancelled. 

Entered value for parameter Process Only Messages with Status as "TBDL, WAIT, HOLD, NDLV". 

Could you please clarify why the job is not cancelling 2020 messages?

Thank you. 

Regards,

Jegatheeswaran P.

entry.title: SAP CPI OAuth2 Authorization Code (Generic) The Provider 'Generic' did not return a refresh token
entry.content:
hi,

on cpi I configured an OAuth2 Authorization Code (Generic) user in the security material

 

but when I click on the "authorize" button I receive this error The Provider 'Generic' did not return a refresh token for the OAuth2 Authorization Code Credential

can someone help me?

entry.title: Enabling Payload message Archiving for SAP NW 750 SP 24 - PO system
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

Our requirement was to enable archiving for Payload messages in SAP PO 7.5 SP24 JAVA system.

As per information and guidelines are available on support portal as well as on SAP blogs, we have enable it on our PO Quality system as part of XML Data Archiving Service for complete adapter engine and our AIX server file system got full (Configuration set on file system) and due to which we need to delete archives manually from AIX O/S end.

Now we are trying execute/generate it again for only Payload related messages and we are getting error as attached in SAP Case and see no messages archived on the file system.

We would need your assistance in proceeding further on this

Question 2: When we initially executed the job and we could see the Messages being archived. But this was archiving all the messages(xi_af_msg). Our requirement is to archive specific  scenario of Payload Messages and tried applying the rule in the configuration for 2nd time when we have executed as mentioned above.

Hence requesting your assistance on this 2 queries/issues which we are facing now.

Regards,

Jahnavi

entry.title: SAP Trading Partner Management non standard messages format
entry.content:
Hi Community,

is it possible to work with SAP Trading Partner Management in SAP Integration Suite with a non standard message format  ? In the specific use case we are getting text line based (kind of CSV) messages and ideally we can also route this traffic via the SAP Trading Partner Management instead of creating sperate custom iflows. Message protocoll would be AS2.

Thanks

Best regards

DL

entry.title: Merging Multiple PDF Attachments into a Single PDF in SAP PI/PO Using Java Mapping
entry.content:
Hello!

Today I will try to explain how we can merge multiple PDF attachment input into single PDF.

In SAP Process Integration/Process Orchestration (PI/PO), efficient handling of document attachments is crucial for streamlined business processes. Often, there's a need to consolidate multiple PDF attachments into a single PDF file for easier management and distribution. In this blog, we'll explore how to achieve this seamlessly using Java Mapping.

Brief requirement: The real life scenario demanded to send a bundle of different third party system generated PDF as an attachment to SAP PI/PO . The multiple PDF should be merged together and send as an merged output to another third party application. 

  1. Understanding Input and Output:

    • Input: Multiple PDF attachments in the message payload.
    • Output: Single merged PDF file.

  2. Java Mapping Implementation:

    • Create a new Java Mapping program in SAP NetWeaver Developer Studio or SAP Integration Suite.
    • Import necessary libraries for PDF handling (e.g., iText).
    • Implement logic to:
      • Iterate through the input PDF attachments.
      • Extract content from each PDF attachment.
      • Append the content to the output PDF file.

 

import java.io.ByteArrayOutputStream;
import java.io.File;
import java.io.IOException;
import java.nio.file.Files;
import java.util.Base64;
import java.util.Collection;

import org.apache.pdfbox.multipdf.PDFMergerUtility;
import org.apache.pdfbox.io.*;

import com.sap.aii.mapping.api.AbstractTransformation;
import com.sap.aii.mapping.api.Attachment;
import com.sap.aii.mapping.api.InputAttachments;
import com.sap.aii.mapping.api.OutputAttachments;
import com.sap.aii.mapping.api.StreamTransformationException;
import com.sap.aii.mapping.api.TransformationInput;
import com.sap.aii.mapping.api.TransformationOutput;
//import com.sap.aii.mapping.api.exception.AttachmentNotFoundException;
import com.sap.engine.interfaces.messaging.api.MessageDirection;
import com.sap.engine.interfaces.messaging.api.MessageKey;
import com.sap.engine.interfaces.messaging.api.PublicAPIAccessFactory;
import com.sap.engine.interfaces.messaging.api.auditlog.AuditAccess;
import com.sap.engine.interfaces.messaging.api.auditlog.AuditLogStatus;

public class PDFMergingUtility extends AbstractTransformation {

    @Override
    public void transform(TransformationInput transformationInput, TransformationOutput transformationOutput)
            throws StreamTransformationException {

        final String DASH = "-";
        String msgID;
        String uuidTimeLow = "";
        String uuidTimeMid = "";
        String uuidTimeHighAndVersion = "";
        String uuidClockSeqAndReserved = "";
        String uuidClockSeqLow = "";
        String uuidNode = "";
        String msgUUID = "";
        AuditAccess audit;
        MessageKey msgKey;

        InputAttachments inputAttachments = transformationInput.getInputAttachments();
        OutputAttachments outputAttachments = transformationOutput.getOutputAttachments();

        try {
            msgID = transformationInput.getInputHeader().getMessageId();
            uuidTimeLow = msgID.substring(0, 8);
            uuidTimeMid = msgID.substring(8, 12);
            uuidTimeHighAndVersion = msgID.substring(12, 16);
            uuidClockSeqAndReserved = msgID.substring(16, 18);
            uuidClockSeqLow = msgID.substring(18, 20);
            uuidNode = msgID.substring(20, 32);
            msgUUID = uuidTimeLow + DASH + uuidTimeMid + DASH + uuidTimeHighAndVersion + DASH + uuidClockSeqAndReserved
                    + uuidClockSeqLow + DASH + uuidNode;
            msgKey = new MessageKey(msgUUID, MessageDirection.INBOUND);
            audit = PublicAPIAccessFactory.getPublicAPIAccess().getAuditAccess();
            audit.addAuditLogEntry(msgKey, AuditLogStatus.SUCCESS, "Java Mapping: Started");

            if (inputAttachments != null && inputAttachments.areAttachmentsAvailable()) {
                Collection<String> collectionIDs = inputAttachments.getAllContentIds(true);
                PDFMergerUtility pdfMerger = new PDFMergerUtility();

                for (String attachmentID : collectionIDs) {
                    Attachment attachment = null;
                    try {
                        attachment = inputAttachments.getAttachment(attachmentID);
                        String contentType = attachment.getContentType();

                        if (contentType.contains("pdf")) {
                            File tempFile = File.createTempFile("temp", ".pdf");
                            Files.write(tempFile.toPath(), attachment.getContent());
                            pdfMerger.addSource(tempFile);
                        }
                    } catch (Exception e) {
                        audit.addAuditLogEntry(msgKey, AuditLogStatus.WARNING,
                                "Attachment not found: " + attachmentID);
                    }
                }

                ByteArrayOutputStream mergedPDFStream = new ByteArrayOutputStream();
                pdfMerger.setDestinationStream(mergedPDFStream);
                pdfMerger.mergeDocuments(null);

                Attachment mergedAttachment = outputAttachments.create("MergedDocument.pdf",
                        mergedPDFStream.toByteArray());

      //Writing merged document as an attachment.
                outputAttachments.setAttachment(mergedAttachment);
                audit.addAuditLogEntry(msgKey, AuditLogStatus.SUCCESS, "Java Mapping: Merged PDF created");
            } else {
                audit.addAuditLogEntry(msgKey, AuditLogStatus.WARNING, "No attachments found");
            }
        } catch (Exception exception) {
            throw new StreamTransformationException(exception.toString());
        }
    }
}

entry.title: SAP PI - REST Receiver PDF Content application/octet-stream
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'm working on a SAP PI interface (synchronous) :
[ABAP Proxy] >>> [SAP PI 7.5] >>> [REST API (REST Adapter)]

I consume a REST API to retrieve a PDF file.
Receiver URL is something like this : https://my-url-api/api/v1/document/{key}/content

The request process is fine, no problem with this part.

The API response is directly the content of the PDF file (application/octet-stream)

Currently I can't retrieve the PDF into my backend ABAP System (ABAP Proxy). 

On my REST communication channel settings I use (Data Format Response):
Data Format : "Binary"
Assign binary message as : "Main Payload of XI Message"
If I use "Attachment to XI Message (No main Payload)", I'm encountering an error like "Logging message failed, due to Failed to log msg" and nothing relevant on PI logs ...

With JSon or XML for Response Data Format It's always the same error : Response does not have the expected format.

With "Main Payload of XI Message", I have this error "Content is not allowed in prolog" or "Content is not allowed in trailing section". 

I also tried without operation mapping on my ICO : In this case no error on SAP PI, and on SXMB_MONI It's also OK, but an error occur on ABAP Code : Error during XML => ABAP conversion: Response Message.
On SXMB_MONI I can open the PDF file via double-click on the Payload.
So I think this is the right way to go... but not sure > How to retrieve correctly the PDF on my ABAP code ?

I also tried some tricks with Error Handling and {http_result}, but always the same problem, PDF content is not allowed on XML tag.

Using Support Attachments on my Communication Channel doesn't change nothing.

If anyone has a solution, I'd love to hear it.

Regards,
Youri

entry.title: How to log a pdf and csv file in CPI which is incoming
entry.content:
I am building a scenario in cpi where csv and pdf files are coming to CPI and being sent to receiver.
before processing the files need to be logged in CPI in original format.
Can someone please help me?

@MortenWittrock can you help please?

entry.title: Integration with Adyen from SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hello All,

Can anyone share if there are any blogs that explains about the integration between Adyen and SAP CPI?

Thanks and Regards,

Naresh Dasika

entry.title: SAP PLEX MES Integration project
entry.content: Hi, I'm a student at the University of Wisconsin, Milwaukee. Working on a SAP PLEX MES integration project using a middleware application called Mendix and majorly on SAP PP module. Here, I wanted to know whether we can be able to pull production order completion status and serial numbers from PLEX MES to SAP system or not? If yes, how & where we can do that?

entry.title: cXML Support in Trading Partner Management of SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Dear all,

today I would like to ask you to vote for the following customer influence request:

Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence (sap.com)

While SAP is heavily working on enhancing the B2B / Trading Partner Management solution on SAP Integration Suite there is one important B2B format still missing and not yet on the roadmap: cXML.

cXML / Commerce XML is an xml-based message protocol to exchange business documents (i.e. purchase order, order confirmation, shipping notification, invoice, etc.) between procurement applications / trading partners. A lot of e-procurement platforms (Ariba, Coupa, Jaegger, etc.) are using this standard. Therefor it would be great to have this also as a part of the B2B solution on SAP Integration Suite.

In case that you do like this improvement request and have similar requirements / pain points, please vote for this improvement to increase the chance that SAP will take this into consideration for the roadmap.

Thanks and best regards!

Christopher

entry.title: SAP PO Alert Rule Connection refused - Couldn't connecto to host
entry.content:
Hi,

I created an Alert Rule on sap po repository. I created a job on NWA, but when I release the job I get the following error:

does anyone know what causes this?

Thank you

entry.title: SAP PI Scenario Sender REST Query or Odata
entry.content:
We are migrating SAP Gateway to SAP PI and there is a scenario where it uses OData <-> RFC, this endpoint used now is similar: <url sap gateway>$filter=Field1 eq 'Data1' and Field2 eq 'Field2'

For cause of application, we wil have to create a scenario to PI using a request with GET https://host:port/RESTAdapter/apitest$filter=Field1 eq 'Data1' and Field2 eq 'Field2' and ... and I see OData Sender channel in PI, but I didn't fiund anything about this subject with, my question: Is it possible to build a similiar endpoint using:

https://host:port/RESTAdapter/apitest$filter=Field1 eq 'Data1' and Field2 eq 'Field2' and ...

in SAP PI? Because we want there to be no incompatibility with the application that now consumes OData Gateway, so we want the url to be modified as little as possible.

entry.title: Integrate Solman and ServiceNow using SAP CPI or PO as middleware.
entry.content:
There is a requirement to Integrate Solman with ServiceNow using a middleware. SNMP Trap is being used at the Solman side. Is it possible to use SAP CPI or PO in this scenario as middleware?
And if SNMP Trap is avoided, is there any different way to send data to middleware?

Any information will be much appreciated.
Thank You.

entry.title: SAP PI with multiple inbound calls
entry.content:
Hi experts.

I have scenario where an external system makes a call to PI, then PI use that information to make a call to SAP and using the information returned from SAP, it makes another call to a third party API that will generate some data, that will passed to the original consumer (the external system). Also, the third party API uses OAuth 2.0, I don't know if this affects the scenario.

Is this scenario possible and/or is there a better aprocach to it? And how I would configure this in an ICO? 

Attached image for clarification

entry.title: Any Online Editor Available for Groovy SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

Are there any alternative online SAP CPI Groovy editors available besides https://groovyide.com/cpi? The https://groovyide.com/cpi server is currently unavailable.

Thanks in Advance

entry.title: Integration possibilities from SAP CI(Integration Suite) to SWIFT???
entry.content:
Hi All,

While i was exploring the integration possibilities from CI to SWIFT had few questions around this as below.

1. If the integration has to be from S4HANA then there is a direct connector available for SWIFT which we can start configuring from SPRO tcode without the need of CI . Is is the best approach comparing to the other available options???

2. If the connector is not available might be S4HANA release issue or for normal ECC system then that case the traditional way of generating the file in AL11 from where CI will pick the drop onto the target system with all sort of security parameters(encryption/signature) maintained. We dont have standard prepackaged content like what we had for SWIFT SWCV in PI/PO??? Also the SFTP 3.0 which was used in PI/PO for SWIFT integration with the module configurations. How it can be accompanied in CI??? Are there any technical glitches???

3. Any other mechanisms for this integrations???

Please do share your thoughts on this.

Thank You.

Regards,

Farooq

entry.title: Event mesh S/4hana on-premise
entry.content:
hi,

I created a queue on the Event Mesh, how can I direct just one event to this queue? For example, when creating the Business Partner on S/4hana on-premise

I saw that it is necessary to insert the topic name of the queue but it doesn't work for me.

but it only works when I enter * but in this case it intercepts all events:

 

Does anyone know what might be missing?

thank you

entry.title: HTML to PDF in SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hello All,

Would it be possible to convert HTML to PDF in SAP CPI?

Regards,

Naresh

entry.title: JMS Receiver channel ping issue.
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

While performing  the receiver ping channel, I am getting below error for Destination Test. Could you please suggest possible issue and solution.  But end to end message flow happing!! 

Error message in channel ping :

Destination TestChecks for a valid temporary destination object

Thanks,

T Chandar
